part.text:
Actually, before creating files, I realize there's an issue: `NewDerivedReader` imports several dependencies from the kms package context, like `wrapping.Wrapper`, `aead.Wrapper`, `multiwrapper.MultiWrapper`. Since it's going to a completely different package namespace (`internal/libs/crypto` instead of `internal/kms`), we need to ensure it can still access those. 

Looking at the imports in internal/session/util.go, I see that this file already imports kms and wrapping, and uses NewDerivedReader. But actually, looking more carefully at util.go's imports, it only imports from `github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/kms` but doesn't specifically need any internal types.

Let me check what types NewDerivedReader actually needs:
- `wrapping.Wrapper` - from `github.com/hashicorp/go-kms-wrapping` ✓ (already in vendor)
- `aead.Wrapper` - from `github.com/hashicorp/go-kms-wrapping/wrappers/aead` ✓ (already in vendor)  
- `multiwrapper.MultiWrapper` - from `github.com/hashicorp/go-kms-wrapping/wrappers/multiwrapper` ✓ (already in vendor)
- `hkdf.New` - from `golang.org/x/crypto/hkdf` ✓ (already in vendor)
- `io.LimitedReader`, `errors.*` - already available

The good news is that NewDerivedReader doesn't use types or helpers from within internal/kms itself, only external dependencies. So the migration should be clean.

part.text:
---
## Goal
- Refactor NewDerivedReader by moving it from github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/kms to github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/libs/crypto; update all imports across repository and verify typechecks

## Constraints & Preferences
- (none)

## Progress
### Done
- Created new pkg internal/libs/crypto.go with NewDerivedReader function definition
- Moved TestNewDerivedReader test to new file internal/libs/crypto_test.go
- Updated 5 importing files to replace kms.NewDerivedReader with crypto.NewDerivedReader:
  - internal/auth/oidc/auth_method.go (added crypto import)
  - internal/auth/oidc/service.go (replaced import path)
  - internal/credential/vault/client_certificate.go (replaced import path)
  - internal/session/util.go (replaced import path)
  - internal/session/util_test.go (replaced import path)
- Updated usage example comment in internal/kms/kms.go to reference crypto package

### In Progress
- Remove function body from internal/kms/kms.go (preserve usage example comment for documentation)
- Verify project typechecks after refactoring

## Blocked
- (none)

## Key Decisions
- Placed new code in internal/libs/ following existing project structure pattern
- Maintained the usage example comment in kms.go to document function still exists as reference in comments
- All imports updated from "github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/kms" to "github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/libs/crypto" for NewDerivedReader usages

## Next Steps
1. Delete NewDerivedReader function body lines 381-410 from internal/kms/kms.go while keeping example comment
2. Run typecheck verification to ensure refactoring maintains compiler correctness

## Critical Context
- NewDerivedReader imports external dependencies (go-kms-wrapping, golang.org/x/crypto) - does not require internal/kms dependencies
- Total of 6 occurrences updated: 5 in code + 1 const op string in kms.go comment/example
- The function signature remains unchanged; only package namespace differs

## Relevant Files
- internal/libs/crypto.go: New implementation location for NewDerivedReader and its test
- internal/kms/kms.go: Usage pattern example preserved in comments, function body to be removed
- internal/auth/oidc/auth_method.go, service.go
- internal/credential/vault/client_certificate.go  
- internal/session/util.go, util_test.go

## Summary of Current Status

### ✅ Completed Work

The main refactoring objective is **complete**: `NewDerivedReader` has been successfully moved from `internal/kms/kms.go` to `internal/libs/crypto.go`. All imports have been updated in the repository:

### ⚠️ Pending Work

The file `internal/kms/kms.go` has syntax corruption from earlier replacement attempts (around lines 20-30). The struct definition for `ExternalWrappers` needs repair:
- Line 24 contains a literal backslash where tab should be
